acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- Parsing invalid combinations (closed without closed_at/by, assignee without assignee_at, or mismatched stamps) fails with WireError::InvalidValue.
- Roundtrip tests ensure wire serialization only emits valid combinations.
- If redundant fields are kept, they are always consistent with the LWW stamps; if removed, the compatibility path is explicit and tested.
description:
Problem
- WireBead carries redundant fields (status + closed_at/closed_by; assignee + assignee_at).
- wire_to_parts ignores closed_at/closed_by/assignee_at entirely and accepts any combination.
- This is a lie: the wire format claims timestamps/actors but the parser doesn't enforce or consume them.

Impact
- Corrupted or inconsistent store data silently loads.
- We can't rely on the compiler to enforce the invariant that redundant fields are consistent with LWW stamps.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and Scatter (invariants live in the reader's head).
design:
Design
- Replace the redundant field trio/quads with typed enums, mirroring beads-core snapshots: WireWorkflow (Open | InProgress | Closed { closed_reason, closed_on_branch, closed_at, closed_by }) and WireClaim (Unclaimed | Claimed { assignee, assignee_at, assignee_expires }).
- Use #[serde(flatten)] to keep the same on-disk field names for compatibility.
- On parse, require that:
  - status=closed implies closed_at and closed_by are present.
  - status!=closed implies closed_* fields are absent.
  - assignee present implies assignee_at present.
  - assignee absent implies assignee_at/expires absent.
- Optionally validate that closed_at/assignee_at match the LWW stamp used for workflow/claim (from _v/default). If they do not, return WireError::InvalidValue.
- If we decide the redundant timestamps are unnecessary, delete them entirely and migrate legacy readers via a compatibility gate (but keep the invariants explicit either way).

Scatter fit
- Make the wire types tell the truth; no hidden redundancy checks at call sites.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Constraint: keep rendering in command files; do not centralize or move rendering logic out of command handlers.

notes.content: Constraint: keep rendering in command files; do not centralize or move rendering logic out of command handlers.

title: git wire: validate workflow/claim redundant fields
